tokui.audiencePlaceholder: For example: railway freshmen, job trainees, beginners
tokui.guidanceGenerateFailed: Teaching guide generation failed. Adjust the lesson goal and try again.
tokui.guidanceExample:
When explaining this concept, do not start with a bare definition. Begin from a real scenario or common misconception so the learner sees why the concept matters.

Teaching sequence:
1. First explain what problem this concept helps solve.
2. Break it into 2-3 key parts, and explain each part with a familiar learner scenario.
3. If the concept is abstract, use an image or step diagram to support the explanation.
4. After the key explanation, stop and ask one understanding-check question.

Interaction requirements:
- The question is not decorative; it must check whether the learner really understands.
- If the learner answers vaguely, ask for a concrete scenario, action, or judgment rule.
- If the learner has a misconception, do not simply reject it. Restate the learner's idea first, then explain the difference.

Feedback requirements:
- Decide the next explanation based on the learner's answer.
- If the answer is correct, add a more professional judgment or applied example.
- If the answer is incomplete, point out the missing step and fill it with a simpler example.

By the end of this node, the learner should be able to restate one clear judgment instead of only reading an explanation.
tokui.guidanceHint: Write a teaching guide for the AI, not a student-facing script. Teachers can start with a rough intent and ask AI to expand it into a detailed guide: sequence, explanation style, checkpoint, follow-up, and feedback rules.
tokui.imageGenerateFailed: Image generation failed. Check configuration or try again later.
tokui.imageGenerateSuccess: Image generated and added to media.
tokui.imageJobFailedHint: Prompt optimization or image generation failed. Adjust the prompt and try again.
tokui.imageJobQueued: Image job created. Select a candidate after generation finishes.

imageJobStatus.awaiting_selection: Candidates are ready. Select one for media.

tokui.imagePromptPlaceholder: Describe the teaching diagram to generate, for example: explain pressure transfer in a railway braking system, clear classroom style
tokui.imageSelectFailed: Failed to select candidate. Try again later.
tokui.blockingInteractionLabel: Blocking: continue after the learner answers
tokui.interactionContinuationPlaceholder: How the answer changes later teaching, for example: choose which railway type case to reinforce
tokui.interactionPointsHint: One lesson can have multiple inputs and checks
tokui.interactionPromptPlaceholder: Describe what to ask and what the question diagnoses
tokui.materialDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe the material or generation prompt, for example: split-screen real footage of four railway types with subtitles
tokui.materialInsertionPlaceholder: Insertion point, for example: immediately after explaining the four railway types
tokui.materialPlacementsHint: Record where materials belong instead of assuming one image
tokui.materialPurposePlaceholder: Purpose, for example: build spatial understanding, support comparison, or correct a misconception
tokui.materialTitlePlaceholder: Material name, for example: Core parameter comparison chart for Chinese railway types

checkpoint.desc: After the learner answers, the learner runtime continues with that answer as context.
checkpoint.title: Checkpoint question

normal.desc: For reflection or simple confirmation when later content does not depend on the answer.
normal.title: Normal interaction

tokui.intentPlaceholder: For example: learners can explain how braking pressure transfers and identify a common misconception
tokui.mediaRefsHint: Resource picker can be connected later
tokui.mediaUrlPlaceholder: Resource URL or stable resource ID
tokui.previewEmpty: Write the teaching guide, then generate a sample preview.
tokui.previewFailedHint: Try regenerating, or make the teaching guide more concrete: sequence, checkpoint question, feedback rules, and media usage.
tokui.previewHint: This is a teacher-side sample preview. After publishing, the learner side dynamically generates from learner progress, saved answers, and context.
tokui.promptPlaceholder:
Describe the teaching guide in detail:

- What scenario or problem should this node start from?
- What definitions should not be dumped directly, and how should the AI guide step by step?
- Which ideas need analogies, examples, images, or videos?
- Where should the AI stop and ask the learner, and what is the purpose of that question?
- After the learner answers, how should the AI give feedback, ask a follow-up, or correct misconceptions?
- How can we tell the learner really learned this node?
tokui.subtitle: Teach the AI how to teach, then let learner-side TokUI render dynamically from context.
tokui.title: TokUI teaching guide

history.confirmDescription: After confirmation, the current lesson content will be fully replaced by this version.
history.confirmLoadFailed: Failed to load this version content. Please try again later.
history.restoreLessonDeleted: This lesson has been deleted by another editor and cannot be restored. Please refresh and try again.
history.title: History

tokuiImageAdmin.apiKeyConfigured: Configured; leave blank to keep it
tokuiImageAdmin.description: Configure TokUI teacher-side async image generation, candidate count, and prompt optimization. API keys are write-only.
tokuiImageAdmin.loadFailed: Failed to load TokUI image configuration
tokuiImageAdmin.optimizerHint: Teachers enter a teaching image intent. The system first asks an LLM to optimize it into a classroom-diagram image prompt. If optimization fails, image generation will not run.
tokuiImageAdmin.providerHint: The image provider is compatible with the OpenAI Images API. The backend sends one single-image request for each candidate.
tokuiImageAdmin.saveFailed: Failed to save TokUI image configuration
tokuiImageAdmin.title: TokUI Image Configuration

previewArea.copyTooltip: Paste it into the teaching prompt on the left, and next time it will teach with this snippet
previewArea.description: Feel the learner’s experience and see how prompts personalize courses
previewArea.empty: Enter teaching prompts on the left, then click "Debug" to experience learning simulation
previewArea.title: Learning Simulation
previewArea.variablesDescription: Different learners set different variables to get different courses
previewArea.variablesHideSingleConfirmDesc: Hide "{name}". You can restore it by clicking "Show all".
previewArea.variablesHideUnusedConfirmDesc: Hide custom variables not used in the course. After hiding, you can click this button to show them again.
previewArea.variablesHideUnusedTooltip: Hide custom variables not used in the course
previewArea.variablesRestoreHiddenConfirmDesc: Show all variables in the course. After showing, you can click this button to hide unused custom variables.
previewArea.variablesRestoreHiddenTooltip: Show all variables in the course
